structs.description: Plays back audio from a Buffer with variable rate, interpolation, looping, and triggering capabilities.

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.summary: Args:     world: pointer to the MMMWorld instance.

args.description: The audio buf to read from (List[MFloat[num_chans]]).
args.name: buf

args.description: The playback rate. 1 is the normal speed of the buf.
args.name: rate

args.description: Whether to loop the buf (default: True).
args.name: loop

args.description: Trigger starts the synth at start_frame (default: 1.0).
args.name: trig

args.description: The start frame for playback (default: 0) upon receiving a trigger.
args.name: start_frame

args.description: The end frame for playback (default: -1 means to the end of the buf).
args.name: num_frames

overloads.name: next

parameters.description: Number of output channels to read from the buffer and also the size of the output SIMD vector.
parameters.name: num_chans

parameters.description: Interpolation method to use when reading from the buffer (see the Interp struct for available options - default: Interp.linear).
parameters.name: interp

parameters.description: Whether to interpolate between the end and start of the buffer when reading (default: False). This is necessary when reading from a wavetable or other oscillating buffer, for instance, where the ending samples of the buffer connect seamlessly to the first. If this is false, reading beyond the end of the buffer will return 0. When True, the index into the buffer will wrap around to the beginning using a modulus.
parameters.name: bWrap

returns.doc: The next sample(s) from the buf as a SIMD vector.

overloads.signature: next[num_chans: Int = 1, interp: Int = Interp.linear, bWrap: Bool = False](mut self, buf: SIMDBuffer[num_chans], rate: Float64 = 1, loop: Bool = True, trig: Bool = True, start_frame: Int = 0, var num_frames: Int = -1) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Get the next sample from a SIMD audio buf (SIMDBuffer). The internal phasor is advanced according to the specified rate. If a trigger is received, playback starts at the specified start_frame. If looping is enabled, playback will loop back to the start when reaching the end of the specified num_frames. A key difference between SIMDBuffer and Buffer is that calling next on a SIMDBuffer always returns the entire SIMD vector of samples for the current phase, whereas with Buffer, you can specify the number of channels to read.

args.description: The audio buf to read from (List[Float64]).
args.name: buf

args.description: The playback rate. 1 is the normal speed of the buf.
args.name: rate

args.description: Whether to loop the buf (default: True).
args.name: loop

args.description: Trigger starts the synth at start_frame (default: 1.0).
args.name: trig

args.description: The start frame for playback (default: 0) upon receiving a trigger.
args.name: start_frame

args.description: The end frame for playback (default: -1 means to the end of the buf).
args.name: num_frames

args.description: The start channel for multi-channel bufs (default: 0).
args.name: start_chan

overloads.name: next

parameters.description: Number of output channels to read from the buffer and also the size of the output SIMD vector.
parameters.name: num_chans

parameters.description: Interpolation method to use when reading from the buffer (see the Interp struct for available options - default: Interp.linear).
parameters.name: interp

parameters.description: Whether to interpolate between the end and start of the buffer when reading (default: False). This is necessary when reading from a wavetable or other oscillating buffer, for instance, where the ending samples of the buffer connect seamlessly to the first. If this is false, reading beyond the end of the buffer will return 0. When True, the index into the buffer will wrap around to the beginning using a modulus.
parameters.name: bWrap

returns.doc: The next sample(s) from the buf as a SIMD vector.

overloads.signature: next[num_chans: Int = 1, interp: Int = Interp.linear, bWrap: Bool = False](mut self, buf: Buffer, rate: Float64 = 1, loop: Bool = True, trig: Bool = True, start_frame: Int = 0, var num_frames: Int = -1, start_chan: Int = 0) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Get the next sample from an audio buf (Buffer). The internal phasor is advanced according to the specified rate. If a trigger is received, playback starts at the specified start_frame. If looping is enabled, playback will loop back to the start when reaching the end of the specified num_frames.

structs.name: Play
structs.summary: The principle buffer playback object for MMMAudio.

structs.description: Used as part of the TGrains and the PitchShift structs for triggered granular synthesis.

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.summary: Args:     world: Pointer to the MMMWorld instance.

args.description: Audio buffer containing the source sound.
args.name: buffer

args.description: Playback rate of the grain (1.0 = normal speed).
args.name: rate

args.description: Whether to loop the grain (default: False).
args.name: loop

args.description: Starting frame position in the buffer.
args.name: start_frame

args.description: Duration of the grain in seconds.
args.name: duration

args.description: Panning position from -1.0 (left) to 1.0 (right).
args.name: pan

args.description: Amplitude scaling factor for the grain.
args.name: gain

overloads.name: next_pan2

parameters.description: Either 1 or 2, depending on whether you want to pan 1 channel of a buffer out 2 channels or 2 channels of the buffer with equal power panning.
parameters.name: num_playback_chans

parameters.description: Type of window to apply to the grain (default is Hann window (WinType.hann)).
parameters.name: win_type

parameters.description: Whether to interpolate between the end and start of the buffer when reading (default: False). When False, reading beyond the end of the buffer will return 0. When True, the index into the buffer will wrap around to the beginning using a modulus.
parameters.name: bWrap

overloads.signature: next_pan2[num_playback_chans: Int = 1, win_type: Int = 0, bWrap: Bool = False](mut self, mut buffer: SIMDBuffer[num_chans], rate: Float64 = 1, loop: Bool = False, start_frame: Int = 0, duration: Float64 = 0, pan: Float64 = 0, gain: Float64 = 1) -> SIMD[DType.float64, 2]
overloads.summary: Generate the next grain and pan it to stereo using pan2. Depending on num_playback_chans, will either pan a mono signal out 2 channels using pan2 or a stereo signal out 2 channels using pan_stereo.

args.description: Audio buffer containing the source sound.
args.name: buffer

args.description: Playback rate of the grain (1.0 = normal speed).
args.name: rate

args.description: Whether to loop the grain (default: False).
args.name: loop

args.description: Starting frame position in the buffer.
args.name: start_frame

args.description: Duration of the grain in seconds.
args.name: duration

args.description: The buffer channel to read from for the grain (default: 0).
args.name: buffer_chan

args.description: Panning position from 0.0 to 1.0.
args.name: pan

args.description: Amplitude scaling factor for the grain.
args.name: gain

args.description: Number of speakers to pan to.
args.name: num_speakers

overloads.name: next_pan_az

parameters.description: Number of output channels (speakers). Must be a power of two that is at least as large as num_speakers.
parameters.name: num_simd_chans

parameters.description: Type of window to apply to the grain (default is Hann window (WindowType.hann) See [WindowType](MMMWorld.md/#struct-windowtype) for all options.).
parameters.name: win_type

parameters.description: Whether to interpolate between the end and start of the buffer when reading (default: False). When False, reading beyond the end of the buffer will return 0. When True, the index into the buffer will wrap around to the beginning using a modulus.
parameters.name: bWrap

overloads.signature: next_pan_az[num_simd_chans: Int = 4, win_type: Int = WindowType.hann, bWrap: Bool = False](mut self, mut buffer: SIMDBuffer[num_chans], rate: Float64 = 1, loop: Bool = False, start_frame: Int = 0, duration: Float64 = 0, buffer_chan: Int = 0, pan: Float64 = 0, gain: Float64 = 1, num_speakers: Int = 4) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_simd_chans]
overloads.summary: Generate the next grain and pan it to N speakers using azimuth panning.

args.description: Audio buffer containing the source sound.
args.name: buffer

args.description: Playback rate of the grain (1.0 = normal speed).
args.name: rate

args.description: Whether to loop the grain (default: False).
args.name: loop

args.description: Starting frame position in the buffer.
args.name: start_frame

args.description: Duration of the grain in seconds.
args.name: duration

args.description: Panning position from -1.0 (left) to 1.0 (right).
args.name: pan

args.description: Amplitude scaling factor for the grain.
args.name: gain

overloads.name: next

parameters.description: Number of output channels to read from the buffer and also the size of the output SIMD vector.
parameters.name: num_chans

parameters.description: Type of window to apply to the grain (default is Hann window (WinType.hann)).
parameters.name: win_type

parameters.description: Whether to interpolate between the end and start of the buffer when reading (default: False). When False, reading beyond the end of the buffer will return 0. When True, the index into the buffer will wrap around to the beginning using a modulus.
parameters.name: bWrap

overloads.signature: next[num_chans: Int = 1, win_type: Int = WindowType.hann, bWrap: Bool = False](mut self, mut buffer: SIMDBuffer[num_chans], rate: Float64 = 1, loop: Bool = False, start_frame: Int = 0, duration: Float64 = 0, pan: Float64 = 0, gain: Float64 = 1) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Generate the next unpanned grain. This is called internally by the panning functions, but can also be used directly if panning is not needed. The grain will always have the channel size of the SIMDBuffer.

structs.name: Grain
structs.summary: A single grain for granular synthesis.

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.signature: __init__(out self, num_grains: Int, max_grains: Int, world: UnsafePointer[MMMWorld, MutExternalOrigin])
overloads.summary: Args:     num_grains: Number of grains to initialize.     max_grains: Maximum number of grains that can be allocated.     world: Pointer to the MMMWorld instance.

overloads.description:
Args:.
    buffer: Audio buffer containing the source sound.
    rate: Playback rate of the grains (1.0 = normal speed).
    trig: Trigger signal (>0 to start a new grain).
    start_frame: Starting frame position in the buffer.
    duration: Duration of each grain in seconds.
    buf_chan: Channel in the buffer to read from.
    pan: Panning position from -1.0 (left) to 1.0 (right).
    gain: Amplitude scaling factor for the grains.
overloads.name: next

parameters.description: Either 1 or 2, depending on whether you want to pan 1 channel of a buffer out 2 channels or 2 channels of the buffer with equal power panning.
parameters.name: num_playback_chans

parameters.description: Type of window to apply to each grain (default is Hann window (WinType.hann)).
parameters.name: win_type

parameters.description: Whether to interpolate between the end and start of the buffer when reading (default: False). When False, reading beyond the end of the buffer will return 0. When True, the index into the buffer will wrap around to the beginning using a modulus.
parameters.name: bWrap

returns.doc: List of output samples for all channels.

overloads.signature: next[num_playback_chans: Int = 1, win_type: Int = WindowType.hann, bWrap: Bool = False](mut self, mut buffer: SIMDBuffer[num_chans], rate: Float64 = 1, trig: Bool = False, start_frame: Int = 0, duration: Float64 = 0.10000000000000001, buf_chan: Int = 0, pan: Float64 = 0, gain: Float64 = 1) -> SIMD[DType.float64, 2]
overloads.summary: Generate the next set of grains. Uses pan2 to pan to 2 channels. Depending on num_playback_chans, will either pan a mono signal out 2 channels or a stereo signal out 2 channels.

args.description: Audio buffer containing the source sound.
args.name: buffer

args.description: Playback rate of the grains (1.0 = normal speed).
args.name: rate

args.description: Trigger signal (>0 to start a new grain).
args.name: trig

args.description: Starting frame position in the buffer.
args.name: start_frame

args.description: Duration of each grain in seconds.
args.name: duration

args.description: Channel in the buffer to read from.
args.name: buf_chan

args.description: Panning position from -1.0 (left) to 1.0 (right).
args.name: pan

args.description: Amplitude scaling factor for the grains.
args.name: gain

args.description: Number of speakers to pan to. Must be fewer than or equal to num_simd_chans.
args.name: num_speakers

overloads.name: next_pan_az

parameters.description: The size of the output SIMD vector. Must be a power of two that is at least as large as num_speakers.
parameters.name: num_simd_chans

parameters.description: Type of window to apply to each grain (default is Hann window (WinType.hann)).
parameters.name: win_type

parameters.description: Whether to interpolate between the end and start of the buffer when reading (default: False). When False, reading beyond the end of the buffer will return 0. When True, the index into the buffer will wrap around to the beginning using a modulus.
parameters.name: bWrap

returns.doc: Output samples for all channels as a SIMD vector.

overloads.signature: next_pan_az[num_simd_chans: Int = 2, win_type: Int = WindowType.hann, bWrap: Bool = False](mut self, mut buffer: SIMDBuffer[num_chans], rate: Float64 = 1, trig: Bool = False, start_frame: Int = 0, duration: Float64 = 0.10000000000000001, buf_chan: Int = 0, pan: Float64 = 0, gain: Float64 = 1, num_speakers: Int = 2) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_simd_chans]
overloads.summary: Generate the next set of grains. Uses azimuth panning for N channel output.

structs.name: TGrains
structs.summary: Triggered granular synthesis. Each trigger starts a new grain.

structs.description:
Args:
    world: Pointer to the MMMWorld instance.
    buf_dur: Duration of the internal buffer in seconds.

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.signature: __init__(out self, world: UnsafePointer[MMMWorld, MutExternalOrigin], buf_dur: Float64 = 2, num_grains: Int = 6, max_grains: Int = 12)
overloads.summary: Args:     world: pointer to the MMMWorld instance.     overlaps: Number of overlapping grains (default is 4).     buf_dur: duration of the internal buffer in seconds.

args.description: Input signal to be pitch shifted.
args.name: in_sig

args.description: Duration of each grain in seconds.
args.name: grain_dur

args.description: Number of overlapping grains (default is 4).
args.name: overlaps

args.description: Pitch shift ratio (1.0 = no shift, 2.0 = one octave up, 0.5 = one octave down, etc).
args.name: pitch_ratio

args.description: Amount of random variation in pitch ratio.
args.name: pitch_dispersion

args.description: Amount of random variation in grain triggering time. Value between 0.0 and 1.0, where 0.0 is no variation and 1.0 is maximum variation of up to the grain duration.
args.name: time_dispersion

args.description: Minimum amount of delay to add to the start of each grain in seconds.
args.name: added_delay_low

args.description: Maximum amount of delay to add to the start of each grain in seconds. (Maximum added delay should be set so that it does not exceed the internal buffer size when combined with the grain duration and time dispersion).
args.name: added_delay_high

args.description: Amplitude scaling factor for the output.
args.name: gain

overloads.name: next
overloads.signature: next(mut self, in_sig: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], grain_dur: Float64 = 0.20000000000000001, overlaps: Int = 4, pitch_ratio: Float64 = 1, pitch_dispersion: Float64 = 0, time_dispersion: Float64 = 0, added_delay_low: Float64 = 0, added_delay_high: Float64 = 0, gain: Float64 = 1) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Generate the next set of grains for pitch shifting.

structs.name: PitchShift

parameters.description: Number of input/output channels.
parameters.name: num_chans

parameters.description: Type of window to apply to each grain (default is Hann window (WinType.hann)).
parameters.name: win_type

structs.signature: struct PitchShift[num_chans: Int = 1, win_type: Int = WindowType.hann]
structs.summary: An N channel granular pitchshifter. Each channel is processed in parallel.
